notes: Tanvi Shah. She is the first Indian Woman to win a Grammy Award. Grammy Award is given in the field of Music. It is presented by the Recording Academy of the United States. First Indian who won a grammy award was Pandit Ravi Shankar. A R Rahman won Two Grammy Awards in 2010. The record for the most Grammy Awards won is held by Beyoncé (USA). Q.714. With which of the following states is the Bhatiali folk music, which is mostly sung by the fishermen, associated ? SSC CHSL 01/07/2024 (1st shift) (a) Kerala (b) Andhra Pradesh (c) West Bengal (d) Odisha Sol.714.(c) West Bengal. Bhatiali music is a river song characterized by its soulful and melancholic tunes, reflecting the lives and struggles of the fishermen community. Other folk music of West Bengal: Baul, Bhawaia, Kirtan, Gambhira, Shyama Sangeet, Lalon Geeti. Other States and their folk music: Kerala - Kathakali Music, Mappila Pattu, Ottamthullal Songs, Sopanam, Kolkali. Andhra Pradesh - Madiga Dappu, Mala Jamidika. Odisha - Avanti, Panchali, Odramagadhi.

Kesari. It is based on the true story of the Battle of Saragarhi in 1897. The film was directed by Anurag Singh. ‘Teri mitti’ song : Writer - Manoj Muntashir. Composer - Arko Pravo Mukherjee. Singer - B Praak.

Pandavani. It is a folk music tradition from Chhattisgarh. It narrates the story of the five Pandava brothers (protagonists of the epic Mahabharata). This age-old musical form was kept alive by performers like Teejan Bai, Jhaduram Devangan, Ritu Verma, Usha Barle and Shantibai Chelak. Folk songs of Chhattisgarh : Fag, Karma songs, Danda songs, Sawnahi, Kau-Mau, Sua songs, Chau-Mau Dewar songs, Cher-Chera songs, Sohar song, Khuduwa, Dhankul songs, Bhojali songs, Dandi Pouha, Loriya, Goura songs, Fugdi etc.

Purandara Dasa. Tamil Nadu is famous for Carnatic music, based on ragas (melodies) and talas (rhythms). Purandara Dasa has contributed significantly to its codification and giving it a clear format for its teaching and performance. The devotional songs composed by Shyama Shastri, Tyagaraja and Muthuswami Dikshitar are very popular. These songs are accompanied by instruments like the mridangam, veena, nadaswaram, and flute. In recognition of its musical heritage, Chennai was included in the UNESCO Creative Cities Network in 2017.

Haryana. Raganis are performed by multiple singers and dancers and are sung in the local dialect. State and their Folk songs : Haryana - Gharwa Gayan, Jhoolana, Patka, and Rasia. Uttar Pradesh - khayal, ghazals, Swang, Naqal. Bihar - Sohar, Bidesia, Chhath Geet, Kajri. Madhya Pradesh - Nirguni Bhajans, Fag songs, Pandavani. Q.719. Match List-I (Name of the composition) with List-II (Writer). List-I (Name of the Composition) List-II (Writer) 1. Vande Mataram A. Pradeep 2. Jana Gana Mana B. Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay 3. Ae Mere Watan Ke Logon C. Rabindranath Tagore SSC CHSL 02/07/2024 (1st shift) (a) 1-C, 2-A, 3-B (b) 1-B, 2-C, 3-A (c) 1-C, 2-B, 3-A (d) 1-A, 2-B, 3-C Sol.719.(b) 1-B, 2-C, 3-A. "Aye Mere Watan Ke Logo" is a patriotic song written in Hindi by Kavi Pradeep, composed by C. Ramchandra, and sung by singer Lata Mangeshkar. Jana Gana Mana is the national anthem of the Republic of India. It was originally composed as "Bharoto Bhagyo Bidhata" in Bengali by polymath Rabindranath Tagore on 11 December 1911. "Vande Mataram" is a Bengali poem by Bankim Chandra Chatterjee. Q.720. Which folk music does NOT belong to Nagaland? SSC CHSL 02/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) Nyioga (b) Heliamleu (c) Hereileu (d) Neuleu Sol.720.(a) Nyioga. It is sung when a marriage ceremony is concluded and the bride's family returns leaving the bride in her home in Arunachal Pradesh. Nagaland's folk music is known for its enchanting melodies and lyrics that tell stories of love, nature, bravery, and cultural identity. Nagaland famous folk dance: Hereileu, Chang Lo, Tetseo Sisters, Tati, Theku, Asem, Kuki dance.

Nagaland. The Hekialeu song is known as the war song of Nagaland. This is because the aged people of Nagaland narrate their achievements in past battles through this form of music. Folk music in India : Baul (West Bengal), Mando (Goa), Sohar (Uttar Pradesh and Bihar), Chhakri (Jammu and Kashmir), and Powada (Maharashtra).

Manipur. Khubakeshei is a traditional folk song from Manipur, typically sung with clapping and accompanied by traditional instruments like the Pena (a stringed instrument) or the Khartal (a pair of cymbals). Popular folk songs of Manipur : Pena Khongba, Khulang Eshei, Lai Haraoba Eshei.

Gitam. The main traditional forms of improvisation in Carnatic music consist of the following: Alapana, Niraval, Pallavi, Ragam, Swarakalpana, Tanam, Tani, Avartanam. Varnam - A type of composition in Carnatic music that captures the essence of a raga, or scale, in a short piece of music. Padas are scholarly compositions in Telegu and Tamil.

Gurajada Apparao. Gurajada Venkata Apparao was an Indian playwright, dramatist, poet, and writer known for his works in Telugu theatre. Rao wrote the play Kanyasulkam in 1892, which is considered as the greatest play in the Telugu language. He holds the titles Kavisekhara and Abyudaya Kavitha Pithamahudu. In 1910, Rao scripted the widely known Telugu patriotic song "Desamunu Preminchumanna".

Wanawun. It is usually performed by women, who sing and dance together, accompanied by traditional instruments like the tarang (a type of drum) and the tumbaknari (a single-headed drum). Other folk music: Alha - Uttar Pradesh and Madhya Pradesh. Panihari - Rajasthan. Baul - West Bengal.

Agra Gharana was founded by Haji Sujan Khan. Agra was an important part of Braj Bhumi, the land of Shri Krishna, and home to Bhakti and Sufi saints like Surdas and Raskhan. The Gwalior Gharana, considered the oldest Khayal Gharana in Indian classical music, was founded by Nathan Khan, and Tansen was one of its earliest and most famous vocalists. Q.727. In classical Carnatic music, tempo is called as ________. SSC CHSL 04/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) padam (b) gita (c) laya (d) achala Sol.727.(c) laya. Carnatic music originated in South India whereas Hindustani music originated in North India. Different forms of Carnatic Music: Gitam (simple composition), Swarajathi (devotional or heroic), Varnam (unique to Carnatic music), Kirtanam (focused on devotion), and Kriti (an evolved form of Kirtanam).

10. These 10 thaats are: Bilawal, Khamaj, Kafi, Asavari, Bhairav, Bhairavi, Todi, Purvi, Marwa, Yaman. Each thaat has a specific arrangement of whole and half steps, and ragas are derived from these thaats by using different combinations of notes, ornamentations, and emotional expressions.

Ekam Satyam. A.R. Rahman collaborated with Michael Jackson on the song "Ekam Satyam" (also known as "The One Truth"), which was recorded in 1999. Michael Jackson ("King of Pop") was an American singer, songwriter, dancer, and record producer.

AR Rahman. Some Patriotic songs and their Singers: ‘Aye mere watan ke logo’ (Lata Mangeshkar), ‘Yeh Desh Hai Veer Jawanon Ka’ (Mohammed Rafi), ‘Bharat Ka Rahnewala Hoon’ (Mahendra Kapoor), ‘Phir Bhi Dil Hai Hindustani’ (Udit Narayan), ‘Aao Bachho Tumhe Dikhaye’ (Kavi Pradeep), ‘Nanha Munna Rahi Hun’ (Shanti Mathur), ‘Ae Watan Tere Liye (Mohammad Aziz & Kavita Krishnamurthy)’, ‘Teri Mitti’ (B Praak), ‘Sandese Aate Hai’ (Sonu Nigam & Roop Kumar Rathod).

Madhya Pradesh. Pai song is sung to pray for a good monsoon and a good harvest. The Saira dance is performed by members of the peasant community to seek the blessings of Lord Indra (rain god) for a good harvest. Other festivals of Madhya Pradesh: Lokrang Festival, Akhil Bharatiya Kalidas Samaroh, Lokranjan or Khajuraho Festival, Bhagoria Haat Festival, Nagaji Fair, and Chethiyagiri Vihara Festival. Q.732. The music of traditional _________consists of thumri and other lyrical song forms. SSC CHSL 05/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) Kathakali (b) Sattriya (c) Kathak (d) Kuchipudi Sol.732.(c) Kathak. The word "Kathak" is derived from "Katha," meaning story. Kathak has three main schools or gharanas-Lucknow, Jaipur, and Banaras-each with its unique emphasis. The Lucknow Gharana focuses on expressiveness and grace, the Jaipur Gharana is known for its strong footwork, and the Banaras Gharana combines elements of both. Q.733. Ohira folk music is primarily associated with one of the popular festivals of which of the following states? SSC CHSL 05/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) Jharkhand (b) Karnataka (c) Assam (d) Kerala Sol.733.(a) Jharkhand. Ohira folk song is dedicated to animals. Folk Music in India: Jharkhand - Domkach, Jhumar. Uttar Pradesh - Rasiya Geet, Kajri, Qawwali. Maharashtra - Lavoni, Pavodas.

Hindustani Classical Music is traditionally passed down through oral traditions in a teacher-student (guru-shishya) relationship within Gharanas (musical schools), which preserve unique styles and reflect community traditions across different regions.

Mando. It is a beautiful dance performance and represents the meeting point of Indian and western traditions. Other fairs and festivals of Goa include: Fatorpa Zatra, Shigmo Festival, Shirgao Jatra, Sao Joao Festival, Sangodd, Chikalkalo, and Tripurari Poornima. Marwar Festival (originally known as the Maand Festival) is celebrated in memory of the heroes of Rajasthan.

Javed Akhtar. His other songs - Kandho Se Milte Hain Kandhe (Lakshya); Ye Jo Des Hai Tera (Swadesh). His awards: Padma Bhushan (2007), Padma Shri (1999). Some patriotic songs and writers: Sare Jahan Se Acha Hindustan Hamara- Muhammad Iqbal, Aye Mere Watan Ke Logo - Kavi Pradeep, Aye Watan Watan Mere Abaad Rahe Tu - Gulzar, I Love My India - Anand Bakshi, Teri mitti - Manoj Muntashir.

Dhrupad is a genre in Hindustani classical music (HCM). Other vocal forms associated with HCM: Khayal, Ghazal, Dhammar, Tarana and Thumri. Main traditional forms of Carnatic music: Alapana, Niraval, Pallavi, Ragam, Kalpnaswaram, Thana.

Rajasthan. 'Pankhida' songs are an integral part of Rajasthani folk culture and are typically sung during agricultural activities like sowing, harvesting, and threshing. The peasants sing and speak while playing algoza and manjira. Indian folk music - Bhavageethe (Karnataka and Maharashtra), Bhangra and Giddha (Punjab), Lavani (Maharashtra), Alha (Madhya Pradesh) and Panihari, Pankhida and Maand (Rajasthan). Q.739. ___________ is a musical composition that expresses devotion through madhura bhakti. SSC CHSL 08/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) Tillana (b) Gita (c) Padam (d) Kriti Sol.739.(c) Padam. A musical composition can refer to an original piece, its structure, or the process of creating it. Tillana is a rhythmic Carnatic piece performed at the end of concerts, focusing on rhythm and tempo. Gita is a hymn, often from texts like the Bhagavad Gita. Kriti, another Carnatic composition, expresses various emotions, not limited to devotion.

Karnataka. It is based on the Sanskrit term Karnataka Sangitam, meaning “traditional” or “codified” music. Gitam: Simple, melodious composition in raga. Suladi: Talamalika with sections in different talas. Jatisavaram: Focuses on rhythmical excellence and Jati patterns. Purandara Dasa (1484-1564) is known as the father of Carnatic music. He was a poet, composer, and mystic from Vijayanagar. He's also known as Karnataka Sangeetha Pitamaha.

Carnatic music in the South Indian languages, is a system of music commonly associated with South India, including the modern Indian states of Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu and portions of east and south Telangana and southern Odisha. Some Carnatic Singers: Dr. Nithyashree Mahadevan, Sudha Raghunathan, K J Yesudas, P Unni Krishnan, M Balamuralikrishna, Semmangudi Srinivasa Iyer, Bombay Jayashri, Aruna Sairam.

Svarajati. It consists of three sections, called Pallavi, Anupallavi and Charanam. The theme is either devotional, heroic or amorous. Dhrupad, Khayal, Tappa, Chaturanga, Tarana, Sargam, Thumri and Ragasagar, Hori and Dhamar are ten main styles of singing in Hindustani music.

Tala. Jati is the classification of a Raag based on the number of notes employed in its Aaroh and Avroh. The Raga is a melodic scale composed of basic seven notes. The exposition preceding the composed verses is called alap. The major vocal forms associated with Hindustani classical music are the khayal, dhrupad, dhammar, Tarana and thumri. Q.744. What is 'Padam' in Carnatic Music? SSC CHSL 09/07/2024 (3rd shift) (a) A love poem (b) A poem about courage (c) A patriotic poem (d) A lullaby Sol.744.(a) A love poem. A type of lyrical composition that often deals with themes of love, devotion, or emotions. It is known for its expressive and emotive qualities. Some key terminologies: raga, tala, krithi, varnam, pallavi, anupallavi, charanam, alapana, javali, tillana, swaram.

The Trinity of Carnatic Music are also known as the three Jewels of Carnatic Music. They are credited with creating a new era in Carnatic music history by bringing about a noticeable change in the existing tradition. Tyagaraja: A Telugu composer known for his Pancharatna Kritis. Muthuswami Dikshitar: A Sanskrit and Manipravalam composer. Syama Sastri: A Telugu and Sanskrit composer. Q.746. With which Gharana is the famous Tarana singer Pandit Rattan Mohan Sharma associated ? SSC CHSL 10/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) Patiala Gharana (b) Agra Gharana (c) Talwandi Gharana (d) Mewati Gharana Sol.746.(d) Mewati Gharana. Rattan Mohan Sharma is an Indian classical vocalist. He has received several awards but the closest to his heart is the title of “Badshah-e-Tarana”. His trendsetting albums - “Gayatri Mantra”. Music Gharana and founder: Gwalior - Nanthan Khan, Agra - Hajisujan Khan, Rangeela - Faiyyaz Khan, Jaipur Atrauli - Alladiya Khan, Kirana - Abdul Wahid Khan.

7 talas in Carnatic music: Dhruva, Matya, Jampa, Ata, Triputa, Rupaka, Eka. Carnatic Music: Composed of a system of Ragam (Raga) and Thalam (Tala), it has a rich history and tradition. It developed in states of Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka.

Sohar. Kohbar: It is painted on the wall of the bride and groom's room as a decoration for performing the puja ritual. It is the oldest example of wall painting tradition in Bihar, Jharkhand and Eastern Uttar Pradesh. States and their folk music: Bihar- Bidesia, Kajari, and Sukar ki Biah. Uttar Pradesh- Birha, Nauka Jhakkad, Kajri, Rasiya etc. Q.749. What is Miyan ki Todi in Hindustani music ? SSC CHSL 10/07/2024 (4th shift) (a) Kirtan (b) Tanam (c) Chhand (d) Raag Sol.749.(d) Raag. Miyan ki Todi, often simply referred to as Todi or Darbari Todi is a Hindustani classical raga which gave its name to the Todi thaat, one of the ten types of classical music according to the musicologist Bhatkhande. Ragas from the Todi raganga (class of ragas) include Bilaskhani Todi, Gujari Todi (also called Gurjari Todi), Desi Todi, Hussaini Todi, Asavari Todi and Bahaduri Todi. Q.750. The famous folk music of Rajasthan that was developed in royal courts is known as: SSC CHSL 11/07/2024 (1st shift) (a) Maand (b) Lavani (c) Sohar (d) Dandiya Sol.750.(a) Maand. It is similar to the thumri and the ghazal. Folk music and dance styles of Rajasthan: Ghoomar, Bhavai, Kachhi Ghodi, Kalbelia, Kathputli. Other folk dance: Lavani is a folk dance and music genre that originated in Maharashtra. Sohar is sung during Childbirth in Bihar. Dandiya (Dandiya Raas) performed during the Navaratri festival in Gujarat.

Darbhanga Gharana. It originated in Darbhanga, Bihar in the 17th-century. It is a gharana of the Hindustani classical music dhrupad thought to have been started by Radhakrishna and Kartaram. Gauhar vani developed the raga or unadorned melodic figures. The Dagarvani emphasized melodic curves and graces. The Khandar vani specialised in quick ornamentation of the notes. Q.752. To which Hindustani music Gharana are the Gundecha brothers related? SSC CHSL 11/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) Patiala Gharana (b) Agra Gharana (c) Dagari Gharana (d) Indore Gharana Sol.752.(c) Dagari Gharana. Umakant and Ramakant Gundecha are together known as the Gundecha Brothers. They received the Sangeet natak academy award in 2017. Hindustani Music Gharanas and founders: Gwalior (Nanthan Khan); Agra (Hajisujan Khan); Rangeela (Faiyyaz Khan); Jaipur Atrauli (Alladiya Khan); Kirana (Abdul Wahid Khan).

ragas. Its schedules: Four praharas (periods) in the first half of the day and four in the second half. Srutis divide the frequencies in an octave into 22 distinct parts. Varnam is a musical form in Carnatic music. In Hindustani music, there are various singing styles, including Dhrupad, Tappa, Chaturang, Ragasagar, Dhamar, Hori, Khayal, Tarana, Sargam, and Thumri. Q.754. The Lucknow Gharana which came into existence in the time of Wajid Ali Shah was developed by who among the following? SSC CHSL 11/07/2024 (3rd shift) (a) Ustad Bakhtawar Khan (b) Pandit Kalka Prasad (c) Pandit Thakur Prasad (d) Pandit Bindawar Prasad Sol.754.(c) Pandit Thakur Prasad. The 19th century saw the golden age of Kathak under the patronage of Wajid Ali Shah, the last Nawab of Awadh, who helped establish the Lucknow Gharana, known for its emphasis on bhava (expression). Exponents of Lucknow Gharana:- Lachhu Maharaj, Shambhu Maharaj, Maya Rao, Damayanti Joshi, Birju Maharaj etc.

Muhammad Iqbal (Allama Iqbal) is the National Poet of Pakistan. Iconic figures and their famous quotes: Bhagat Singh - “Inquilab Zindabad”. Subhash Chandra Bose - “Jai Hind”, “Tum Mujhe Khoon Do, Mai Tumhe Azadi Dunga”. Mahatma Gandhi - “Karo ya Maro”. Bankim Chandra Chatterjee - “Vande Mataram”. Pandit Madan Mohan Malviya - “Satyameva Jayate”.

Ali Akbar Khan (Sarod player). He was an Indian Hindustani classical musician of the Maihar gharana. His other Album name : Garden of Dreams, In Concert 1972, The 80 Minute Raga, Shree Rag. His awards : Padma Vibhushan (1989), Padma Bhushan (1967). Other Indian sarod players : Amjad Ali Khan, Amaan Ali Khan, Satyaki Banerjee.

Gulzar. His awards : Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 2013; Padma Bhushan in 2004; Sahitya Akademi Award for Urdu in 2002. His other Poems - Triveni, Raat Pashmine Ki, Suspected Poems. Anand Bakshi - Nagme, Kisse, Baatein, Yaadein. Gurmel Singh - Economic Liberalisation and Indian Agriculture. Shailendra - Na Bairi Na Koi Begana. Q.758. Who was the singer of the famous song 'Mere Desh Ki Dharti Sona Ugle' from the film 'Upkar' (1967) ? SSC MTS 08/09/2023 (3rd Shift) (a) KJ Yesudas (b) Suresh Wadkar (c) Manna Dey (d) Mahendra Kapoor Sol.758.(d) Mahendra Kapoor (Indian playback singer) received Padma Shri (1972). Famous Patriotic songs: "Hai preet jahan ki reet sada, main geet wahan ke gata hoon" (Mahendra Kapoor), “Des rangila" (Mahalakshmi Iyer), "Maa tujhe salam" (AR Rahman), "Kar chale hum fida jaan-o-tan saathiyon” (Mohammed Rafi), "Aye mere pyare watan, aye mere bichchde chaman, tujhpe dil qurban" (Manna Dey).

Rabindranath Tagore. Rabindra Sangeet is a set of folk songs sung during festivals and rituals in West Bengal. Folk songs in West Bengal : Bhatiali, baul. Q.760.Who is credited with creating the popular Raga Miya Ki Malhar? SSC CHSL 17/08/2023 (2nd shift) (a) Tansen (b) Amir Khusrau (c) Meera Bai (d) Zakir Hussain Sol.760.(a) Tansen. Compositions - Darbari Kanhra (night raga), Mian Ki Todi (morning raga), Mian ki Sarang (mid-day raga). Miya ki Malhar (seasonal raga).
Carnatic Music : It is accompanied by instruments such as the flute, veena, nadaswaram, mridangam and ghatam. Purandaradasa is considered to be the father of Carnatic music.
Bihu songs. It celebrates the life - sustaining character of the Brahmaputra. Bhuta song of Kerala is based on superstitions to send away evil ghosts and spirits.

Persia. Qawwali is religious Sufi music that celebrates devotion and one's relationship with God. Amir Khusrau is the popularly acknowledged father of Qawwali. Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan recognised as "Shahenshah-e-Qawwali".

Both (I) and (II). The thumri is another North Indian vocal form and is based on the romantic-devotional literature inspired by the bhakti movement. The text is usually derived from the Radha-Krishna theme and is of primary importance. Q.765. ______ is a vocal form of music from the state of Punjab. SSC CGL 13/04/2022(Morning) (a) Qawwali (b) Ghazal (c) Tappa (d) Bhajan Sol.765.(c) Tappa. The ghazal is a form of amatory poem or ode, originating in Arabic poetry. Bhajan refers to any devotional song with a religious theme or spiritual ideas, specifically among Indian religions, in any language.
Maharashtra. Famous folk music of Maharashtra are Bhajan, Bharud, Gondhal, Kirtan, Lalita, Abhangas and Tumbadi singing.

Bhawaiya. Chalo (folk song of Haryana). Baul (Bengal), Lavani (Maharashtra), Bhavgeet (Karnataka), Nattupura Pattu (Tamil Nadu), Zeliang (Nagaland), Maand (Rajasthan), Kajari (Uttar Pradesh, Bihar), Baryi (Arunachal Pradesh).
Maharashtra.

Abhangs are sung during the pilgrimage to the temples of Pandharpur, by the devotees. Tevaram - The Ancient Tamil Shaivite Devotional Hymns of Tamil Sangam Period. Shabads are devotional songs of the Sikhs sung in gurdwaras or religious occasions.
Vishnu. The term padavali literally means "gathering of songs".

Six. There are around 83 ragas in Indian classical music. Painting/ Dress/ Tribes Q.773. Elelakkaradi dance of Kerala is performed by which of the following tribes? SSC CGL 19/09/2024 (3rd Shift) (a) Irular tribe (b) Kurumba tribe (c) Adiyan tribe (d) Mavilan tribe Sol.773.(a) Irular tribe. Elelakkaradi : The dance resembles the fight of the people against the wild bears which often attack their tribal hamlets.

Sri Lanka. Some tribes from different countries : Nepal - Tamang, Newar, Tharu, Rai, Limbu, and Bhutia. Myanmar - Burmese, Rakhine, Kachin, Danu, Lisu. Bhutan - Monpa, Brokpa, Bumthangpa, Tibetans. Pakistan - Pashtuns, Sindhis, Muhajirs.
Rajasthan. The Raika tribe is renowned for herding animals, particularly camels, sheep, and goats. Other tribes of Rajasthan: Minas, Damor, Dhanka, Garasia, Kathodi etc.

Maasai. The Maasai are a semi-nomadic ethnic group living in southern Kenya and northern Tanzania, near the shores of Lake Victoria and other African Great Lakes." Region wise Tribes of India : Western region - Bhil, Gond, and Koli; South region - Toda, Kurumba, and Irula; Eastern region - Munda, Santhal, and Oraon; Central region - Baiga, Muria, Gonds. Q.777. Manipur's ____________ tribe celebrates the 'Gaan-Ngai' festival, which is a post-harvest festival of the tribe living in the regions of Manipur. SSC CHSL 09/07/2024 (3rd shift) (a) Chothe (b) Zeliangrong (c) Inpui (d) Gangte Sol.777.(b) Zeliangrong. Gaan-Ngai is celebrated by the Zeliangrong tribe of Manipur, Nagaland, and Assam. The festival usually falls in December or January and is a time of feasting, dancing, and merry-making. Some harvest festivals celebrated in India: Uttarayan (Gujarat), Maghi (Punjab), Magha Saaji (Himachal Pradesh), Khichdi (Uttar Pradesh), Pongal (Tamil Nadu).
Bodo. Other tribes and their dances : Khasi - Nongkrem dance. Garo - Wangala dance. Karbi - Domahi Kikang dance.

Sol.779. (a) Eastern. Santhal community is primarily found in the states of Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Bihar. Tribes in five distinct regions are : Himalayan region (Gaddi, the Jaunsari, the Naga, etc.), Middle India (Munda, the Santhal), Western India (Bhil, the Grasia), South Indian Region (Toda, the Chenchu) and the Islands Region (with tribes like the Onge in Bay of Bengal). Highest Scheduled Tribes Populated state - Madhya Pradesh.

North-eastern. Tribes of Meghalaya: Garo, Khasi, Jaintia. Region wise Tribes of India : Western region - Bhil, Gond, and Koli; South region - Toda, Kurumba, and Irula; Eastern region - Munda, Santhal, and Oraon; Central region - Baiga, Muria, Gonds.

Dang (Gujarat). Major tribes:- Tamil Nadu - Irulas, Badagas, Todas, Kotas, Kurumbas; Andhra Pradesh -Gadaba, Andh, Sadhu Andh, Thoti; Arunachal Pradesh - Apatanis, abor, Dafla, Galong, Momba, Tagin; Assam - Bodo, Chakma, Dimasa, Hajong, Garos, Khasis. Major facts: The Gond and Bhil comprise the largest tribal group of India. The smallest tribal community is the Andamanese.

Bhil. They are considered as one of the oldest tribes in India. They were mainly found in parts of Rajasthan, Gujarat, Malwa, Madhya Pradesh and Bihar. Pahari Korwa is mainly concentrated in Jashpur, Sarguja and Raigarh districts of Chhattisgarh. They are a branch of the Kolarian tribe and speak Mundari language.

Maharashtra. Warli painting is a form of tribal art mostly created by the tribal people from the North Sahyadri Range in Maharashtra. Other folk paintings - Bihar (Madhubani Paintings), Odisha (Pattachitra Art), Gujarat (Pithora Painting), Andhra Pradesh (Kalamkari Paintings), West Bengal (Kalighat Pat Art), Miniature Painting (Rajasthan), Tanjore Painting (Tamil Nadu), Gond Painting (Madhya Pradesh), Phad (Rajasthan), Cheriyal Scrolls (Telangana). Q.784. Madhubani style of painting originated in the state of ________ SSC CHSL 15/03/2023 (2nd Shift) (a) Gujarat (b) Karnataka (c) Bihar (d) Rajasthan Sol.784.(c) Bihar. Madhubani paintings (Mithila Art): features - Made by using natural extract like powdered rice and paint using fingers, bamboo twigs, cotton rags. Other famous paintings: Gujarat - Rabari Embroidery, Pithora Paintings; Karnataka - Mysore painting; Rajasthan - Phad Paintings.
Buddhist. Thangka Painting is a Tibetan Buddhist Painting painted on cotton and silk cloths. Kalighat Painting - Calcutta.
Haryana. Mata Mansa Devi Temple, Kalayat Kapil Muni Temple, and Havelis at Bhiwani possess illustrious examples of such paintings.

Jharkhand. Paitkar painting is a folk painting found in East India in the form of scrolls. The Paitkar painting occurs in the bordering areas of Jharkhand, West Bengal and Orissa and is considered as the oldest tribal painting of India.

Margomkali is a ritual folk art of the Syrian Christians of Kottayam and Thrissur districts.
Kashmiri goat. The Changthangi goat of Ladakh which produces the finest fibre or cashmere also called pashmina wool. The best quality pashmina shawls are found in Kashmir.
